heading: Inapplicability of chapter to certain seizures
label: 1974 Inapplicability of chapter to certain seizures
text: The provisions of this chapter shall not apply with respect to a seizure made by a country at war with the United States or a seizure made in accordance with the provisions of any applicable convention or treaty, if that treaty or convention was made with advice and consent to the Senate and was in force and effect for the United States and the seizing country at the time of the seizure.1So in original. Probably should be “of”.1
notes: Amendments1984— substituted “any applicable convention or treaty, if that treaty or convention was made with advice and consent to the Senate and was in force and effect for the United States and the seizing country at the time of the seizure” for “any fishery convention or treaty to which the United States is a party”.Pub. L. 98–364

Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by applicable with respect to seizures made after , by foreign countries of vessels of the United States, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 98–364section 303(c) of Pub. L. 98–364section 1972 of this titleApr. 1, 1983
